description: In Swift, the Swift toolchain version, the SDK/platform, and the build configuration are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In Swift, the Swift toolchain version, the SDK/platform, and the build configuration define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the Swift toolchain version, the SDK/platform, and the build configuration or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the Swift toolchain version, the SDK/platform, and the build configuration, then run swift build -warnings-as-errors and the Xcode static analyzer and swift build (or the Xcode scheme) for the right platform and configuration to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical swift build -warnings-as-errors and the Xcode static analyzer output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
